runway_notes.06:
RWY 06 - right-hand pattern.
Pattern altitude 2400 ft (1000 ft AGL, elev 1411 ft).
Arrival sectors: via E for inbound from East (e.g. from LSZH), direct E1 for shorter approach when already West of E.
Noise abatement: avoid overflying Solothurn city to the south-east.
runway_notes.24:
RWY 24 - left-hand pattern.
Pattern altitude 2400 ft (1000 ft AGL, elev 1411 ft).
Arrival via VRP W (sector West, mandatory report) then HW.
FAF 1.5 NM final, intercept centerline at 2000 ft.
Visual reference only - hand-fly the pattern.

metadata.source: Skyguide AIP / VAC LSZG (VRP names confirmed from public AIP); coordinates are plugin-author approximations
metadata.notes: VRP names (E, E1, HE, HW, W, S, ABM ALTREU) are taken from the published Swiss AIP and are real-world facts. Coordinates here are plugin-author approximations and MUST be cross-checked against the current Skyguide VAC LSZG before merge — they are NOT copied from any commercial source. When the user has a Navigraph subscription installed (Custom Data/cycle_info.txt present), the optional NavigraphSource layer overrides these coordinates at runtime with more precise values; Navigraph data never enters this repository. Pattern direction (06=right, 24=left) confirmed by thWelly from on-site observation: traffic turns onto downwind on the south-east side over the Aare bend. Arrival routes are plausibility-based suggestions and need VAC verification.
